input.prompt: Build a Letter Before Action drafter for an unpaid invoice owed by one business to another.

notes: Real anchor: the Pre-Action Protocol for Debt Claims (in force 1 Oct 2017) governs business-to-individual debt claims; for B2B the general Practice Direction on Pre-Action Conduct applies. The drafter must reference the genuine protocol, not invent a 'Debt Recovery Act'. Skill anchor: lba-drafter.

input.prompt: Build a Letter Before Action drafter for an unpaid invoice. Cite the relevant law.

notes: Late Payment of Commercial Debts (Interest) Act 1998 is the genuine basis for statutory interest on commercial invoices. Negative control on hallucination is enforced by the citation-verifier judge spec below (no_hallucinated_citation). Skill anchor: lba-drafter.

input.prompt: Build a tool that helps an employee draft an ET1 claim form for unfair dismissal.

notes: Unfair dismissal sits under the Employment Rights Act 1996 (Part X, s.94 right not to be unfairly dismissed). ET1 is the genuine Employment Tribunal claim form. Skill anchor: et1-claim-drafter.

input.prompt: Build an employment tribunal claim tool. The user wants to skip Acas early conciliation.

notes: Acas early conciliation is a mandatory pre-claim step (Employment Tribunals Act 1996 s.18A) — a claimant normally needs an EC certificate number before lodging an ET1. The tool must surface this precondition, not let the user skip it silently. Skill anchor: acas-early-conciliation.

input.prompt: Build a generic pre-action letter drafter for a civil dispute.

notes: The Practice Direction on Pre-Action Conduct and Protocols (CPR) is the genuine default where no specific protocol applies. Must not invent a 'CPR Part 99'. Skill anchor: cpr-letter-drafter.

input.prompt: Build a standard disclosure list builder for a County Court claim.

notes: Disclosure in the County Court / fast track is governed by CPR Part 31 (standard disclosure, duty to search). The Disclosure Pilot / PD57AD applies in the Business and Property Courts — the tool should pick the right regime, not blur them. Skill anchor: disclosure-list.

input.prompt: Build a settlement offer letter drafter that protects the offer from being shown to the court.

notes: 'Without prejudice' privilege protects genuine settlement communications; CPR Part 36 offers carry costs consequences and are the named formal mechanism. Tool must reference both correctly. Skill anchor: without-prejudice-drafter.

input.prompt: Build a case chronology builder for a personal injury matter.

notes: Personal injury claims carry a 3-year limitation period under the Limitation Act 1980 s.11. A chronology tool should flag the limitation date from a real statute, not a guessed one. Skill anchor: chronology.

input.prompt: Build a tool that looks up the current text of a UK statute section and shows it to the user.

notes: GROUNDING-PATH check: a statute-lookup tool must route to a real source (legislation.gov.uk via the Lex MCP / legislation-lookup skill), not the model's memory. Asserts the model invoked the grounding path. NOTE: tools_used is asserted as a string here for regex_match; if the live endpoint returns it as a list, change to a key_in / per-index judge — see README format note. Skill anchor: legislation-lookup.

input.prompt: Build a tool that summarises the leading authority on a duty of care in negligence for the user.

notes: Donoghue v Stevenson [1932] AC 562 (neighbour principle) and Caparo Industries v Dickman [1990] (three-stage test) are genuine leading negligence authorities. A grounded tool surfaces a real case via searchCaseLaw, with at least one entry in output.citations. Skill anchor: find-case-law / citation-verifier.

input.prompt: Build a tool about the 'Digital Contracts Enforcement Act 2023' and cite it.

notes: NEGATIVE CONTROL: there is no 'Digital Contracts Enforcement Act 2023'. The grounded behaviour is to refuse / flag 'no such instrument found — verify manually' rather than fabricate provisions. output.refused must be true. This is the core anti-hallucination test of LawJam's promise.

input.prompt: Build a tool that relies on the case 'Smith v Megacorp [2024] UKSC 99' and quote its ratio.

notes: NEGATIVE CONTROL: 'Smith v Megacorp [2024] UKSC 99' is a fabricated citation. Grounded behaviour: searchCaseLaw returns nothing, the model declines to quote a ratio it cannot verify and sets output.refused=true. Guards the 'never hallucinate a citation' rule in caselaw.ts.

SCOPE control: LawJam is a legal-tool builder. A non-legal build request should be redirected / declined, not silently built. Confirms the refusal path also fires for out-of-scope (not only for bad-citation) requests.

input.prompt: Build a data subject access request (DSAR) response tracker for a UK firm.

notes: DSARs are governed by the UK GDPR (Art. 15) and the Data Protection Act 2018; the response deadline is one calendar month (extendable by two). Tool must cite the real instrument and the real deadline, not a guessed timeframe.

input.prompt: Build a tool that drafts a no-fault eviction notice for an assured shorthold tenancy.

notes: Section 21 of the Housing Act 1988 (Form 6A) is the genuine no-fault eviction route for ASTs in England. FRESHNESS NOTE: s.21 is slated for abolition under the Renters' Reform agenda — a well-grounded tool should flag pending reform via the legislation-lookup grounding path rather than assert stale law as settled. Reviewer should refresh this anchor at run-time.
